ir_specs.statement: Linux supports both POSIX reliable signals (hereinafter "standard signals") and POSIX real-time signals.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Linux supports both POSIX reliable signals (hereinafter "standard signals") and POSIX real-time signals.

ir_specs.statement: Signal dispositions Each signal has a current disposition , which determines how the process behaves when it is delivered the signal.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Signal dispositions Each signal has a current disposition , which determines how the process behaves when it is delivered the signal.

ir_specs.statement: The entries in the "Action" column of the table below specify the default disposition for each signal, as follows: Term Default action is to terminate the process.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: The entries in the "Action" column of the table below specify the default disposition for each signal, as follows: Term Default action is to terminate the process.

ir_specs.statement: Ign Default action is to ignore the signal.
ir_specs.source_quote_or_summary: Ign Default action is to ignore the signal.
